notes.m1_3_notes:
Access to programmes shared.
Walked through Grade 7 Matific as a learner. Experienced how programme moves to higher levels, rewards/acknowledges right answers and corrects and gives opportunities when responses are wrong. 

Met with Tshego who talked me through eQuiz. Strategic intent, What it tests, when it is done.
Discussed with EGRA & EGNA. Comfortable with the information it provides and how that information can be used. Need to see Click's external reporting on how we report on progress of the programme.
notes.m1_3_evidence: Met Tshego 17 June 2026 - talked through the programmes and the assessment tools.
notes.m1_4_notes:
Visit to 2 ES schools that are not performing well (usage).
Challenges at the schools electricity, timetabling mainly for Intesen & educators not being convinced. 
School visits indicated key item:

What educators requested:
- reports indicating the impact on performance of learners
- what the programme covers and it's alignment to CAPs

Educator training will be done to ensure they are comfortable with the alignment to CAPS.
While the programme mapping to CAPS is being approved share what the programme includes e.g. peak one covers letters A - E phonetically.
Facilitators need a forum to share ideas and ways of making it interactive and enjoyable for the learners. Facilitators are a big contributor to the success of the programme.
Principles that support the programme also make a big difference. 

Akani Primary school in GC district - School performing well driven by the Principle
Principle indicated that the programme is an opportunity for the learners to get exposure to digital learning and although they are playing games they are gaining knowledge and learning through using the tablets and laptops. Stated the opportunity has been shared to the learners and she intends to do the most with the opportunity for the benefit of her learners.
notes.m1_4_evidence:
Visits - Thembalethu Primary in Katlehong & Tshwaragano Primary School in Thokoza
Output: Engaged with the Head of EdTech on the reporting possibilities to engage teachers on their request

18 June : Akani Primary School
CAPS mapping to the programme will assist in the concerns of the ATP. 
The programme outcomes in whether there is a difference in the learner's performance; reading comprehension and numeracy skills is critical in advocating and influencing for dosage.
notes.m1_5_evidence:
ICT Meeting notes - Ekhurhuleni South
04/06/2026

Gauteng partnership - 11 years
Direct relationship with the province and aeas on our programme.
Justification for the double dosage request. More hours in EC test indicated that there is correlation more time spent the more there are improvements in reading comprehension.

Challenges:
Call to action to the district - power outages & connectivity

Footprint decrease from 31 to 29 What affected the footprint decrease?
Click at private schools?

Deliberate in servicing under resource schools  Quintile 1 - 3 schools in active provinces.
Source out products that fit within SA context and deploy to the schools. Therefore there are products used by Click that are deployed by other service providers to other quintile schools. 
Target time spent on the products 1.5 - 2hrs based on the performance targets. Remediation assistance where there is underperformance  - where there is no remediation (e.g. labs are offline for a number of months) assisting then the partnership is dissolved. Need to report back to funders on the performance of the programme. 

Letters sent to principles and not to the districts (districts wree not aware of the process that the letters were issued) schools were calling the districts. Uncomfortable with the letters sent to the schools - district interviewed schools on reason for discomfort on the letters - emanated from the 2hrs required for them to sign, but CAPS timetable do not have the 2hrs and that caused them to drop the programme. Reason to drop that is coming up - Clicks phonics are not in line with what they are teaching and the phonics was meant to be reinforcing. I.e. - the programme is not reinforcing. 

Gauteng engagement - to implement the 2hr sandbox schools ahd committed to 1 hr. Engaged with schools for the double dosage and they had committed to those hours in 2025. Province requested clarity on the hours and the differences which then yielded resolution to engage with districts on the dosage to stabalise the committed hours. We monitor on the committed dosage per school. Can call a meeting with district on the detail of the letter discomfort - what dosage has each school committed to , what is the performance, where there is underperformance what are the issues and how do we resolve. 

Close the learning gaps - sequencing may be covering prior year gaps. Learner led adaptive model based on what the 

Need to create time to discuss the footprint - ES will advise on how to proceed with that.

notes.checkin_ci_w2_notes: Engagements with the internal stakeholders has provided a lot of insight. Picked up transversal messaging and pain points in the implementation. Build glossary that has assisted in learning internal jargon. There has been a lot of information to weave together which has been helpful in learning Click.
notes.checkin_ci_w4_notes:
This week included attending June Jam which was critical to building the language to articulate what Click is about. Great opportunity to build rapport with all internal stakeholders predominately EXCO and the Provincial HoIs and PLs. 
Got exposure to all units of Click and where each function fits. 
Experienced the strategy articulation and the expectations for the Gov Partnerships role.

log.title: One month review
log.notes:
Gained much insight on the overall service offering of Click. June Jam was great opportunity to hear first hand the what at the mission truely is, the articulation of the impact and intention to crystallise and make the impact statement succinct. Overview of the operations and the other areas of Click was also insightful. 
GR is main enabler of the strategy going forward and intention of role is clear.

log.title: Nomtha - Growth Lead
log.notes:
Districts with what the 
Growth will go into schools and access eagerness and readiness 42 schools and 23 look like quick wins - budget for 220. Reach 1 million learners - moving in the thousands every year. So instead of expanding moving to scale with government.
Started looking at what this looks like, what would be needed. how gov would incorporate.
Work on strategic alignment. Double click endorsed by government - need to have evidence that the programmes are the best - to test assumptions and what is best suited at the grades e.g. reading eggs at gr1 -2 and Amira for grad 3. 2023 double click in EC for literacy and GP for numeracy. Gave them all the resources Growth lead at EC. Consolidation/standerdisation project back to access active schools to ensure the experience at all schools are the same. Fixed the active schools standard e.g. adding labs, laptops - end of 2024 until begin of this year T1. 

EC project BCM 20 schools - start off the scaling 100 - 150 in next year. 
Growth will be the contact for the schools to be implemented. 
We have not agreed on the dosage for the entry schools - testing the dosage at 4hrs on the programme
Shift. Not agreeing as gov to the hours on dosage - Funders are already on board.

log.title: Keagan - Head of Infrastructure
log.notes:
Overview on the new schools (growth) process.
What IFS covers from when a new school has been identified and what GR needs to agree with IFS for them to then go in. The number of labs and devices needs to be agreed before IFS does their part of ensuring the school is fully enabled, i.e. structure, devices, security all of which is then provided before handing over to implementation.

log.title: Nick - National programme manager
log.notes:
Overview of the implementation and how it occurs across. 
Discussed the stakeholder engagements and the different provinces. 

Went through the National Master excel sheet with all the school data. 
Gained insight on the work done with Ayanda in implementation.

Nick shared the work being done in Gauteng on the current struggling schools and the intended interventions required for the top 3 struggling schools. Agreed to have an engagement/workshop with all HoIs in order to work through what and how provincial interactions have been taking place. 
This will be a formal structure for engagement between the GR and Implementation for the GR initiatives.

log.title: Lisa - WC HoI & Sibulele - MP HoI
log.notes:

Discussed their provinces and how GR will interface with their roles. 

With Sibu she shared on the upcoming intensives.
Have established fortnightly touch points with Sibu for regularly engaging on the MP environment. 
Scheduled to meet on the MP intensives.

log.title: Tshego - Research & Innovation
log.notes:
Met 17 June

Learner Assessments 
- EQuiz
- EGRA (Rolls Royce of assessments)
- EGNA
- Annual National Assessments
Universal prvince testing is expensive
EGRA is the standard accepted assessment for testing skill. The validity of the test and tried and tested. 

EQuiz is inhouse and there is work being done to ensure that the testing domains/testing areas/content areas are testing comparativley to EGRA as well as whether the data is valid from testing digitally as opposed to paper based. 

EFRA is subcontracted to data specialists. Is paper based. The assessment has high costs. 
Tests skill eg. receptive, Identification, productive, reading comprehension. 

If Equiz is validated to what the EGRA assessment is able to test it will allow Click to:
- Talk more confidently on the results/outcomes
- Have a cost effective way of assessing 
- Digitized way of assessing
- Can provide them with performance results
